definition: Grimoire recombines generalized XML namespace constructs (xmlns:prefix declarations, bare xmlns= default-namespace attributes, prefixed elements) into consistent multi-fragment documents at high frequency, driving parser namespace-handling paths (xmlNewNs, xmlFreeNs, xmlSearchNs, xmlNsDumpOutput, fullelemname) that cmplog cannot reach because it fails to synthesize the required co-dependent prefix+element structure.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with GAP placeholders and structural mutators splice/recurse them, generating hundreds of valid namespace declarations per execution and repeatedly driving non-xml prefixes through the xmlNewNs gate that cmplog cannot assemble.
signature.one_line: Grimoire generates hundreds of valid namespace declarations per run via structural splicing

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching xmlNewNs's false branch requires a well-formed XML doc with a non-xml namespace declaration parsed through the SAX2 attribute path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed nested XML document (DOCTYPE + nested elements with namespace-bearing attributes) so the SAX2 start-element path repeatedly invokes xmlNewNs for valid non-xml prefixes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's generalization + structural splicing recombine generalised token/gap structures into deeply valid XML; its winning seeds show GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator provenance and a branch hitcount of 1959 (vs loser 1), i.e. it manufactures hundreds of valid namespace declarations per execution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) has I2S but no structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the nested well-formed element structure that produces many xmlNewNs calls — it stays blocked in 8/10 trials with hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on those generalised structures, building grammar-valid nested XML that the cmplog baseline (same I2S, no generalization) cannot construct.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the prefix=='xml' test in xmlNewNs; the winners take the false branch.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
xmlNewNs is reached through xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at SAX2.c:1214 on a winner-only path.
The path to xmlNewNs passes through multi-byte 'xmlns' character-equality gates.
value_profile (pair 3) raises branch hitcount to 89 vs naive's 4 while resolving 9/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=89  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog (pair 1) resolves 10/10 and raises hitcount to 15 vs cmplog's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=15  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand closeness and preserves inputs that get closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire (pair 2) drives branch hitcount to 1959 vs cmplog's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1959  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire winning seeds carry Grimoire structural-mutator provenance.
grimoire's single-technique delta over cmplog is generalization plus Grimoire structural mutators (both have I2S).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice grammar-free structural mutations over generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx (pair 4) resolves 8/10 vs naive 2/10 and raises hitcount to 79 vs 4.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=79  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds call-context into the edge-map index so the same edge is new coverage per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: For naive_ctx a new bucket is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses only naive's havoc/token mutators with no I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating xmlNewNs behind the 'xmlns' string-equality CMPs in which value_profile fails to resolve the false branch faster than naive/cmplog (no resolution-rate gain from the CMP_MAP distance gradient).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L byte divergence, so the specific input bytes that distinguish the three winning techniques are inferred from source/hitcount rather than directly diffed.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire recombines xmlns attribute and valid XML/DOCTYPE scaffolding as generalized token/gap structures, repeatedly constructing the default-namespace path with NULL prefix at extreme hitcount (1959 vs 1) that cmplog's I2S-plus-havoc stack cannot structurally assemble.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices xmlns default-namespace structure to drive NULL-prefix path at high frequency

analysis.summary_one_line: A default-namespace attribute (xmlns="..." with no prefix) parsed deep in SAX2, so xmlNewNs is called with prefix==NULL and takes the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with an element bearing a default namespace declaration `xmlns="..."`; the surrounding DOCTYPE/element scaffolding must be intact enough that the SAX attribute-internal path runs and calls xmlNewNs with NULL prefix.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire generalizes corpus entries into token/gap structures and splices them, so it recombines the `xmlns` attribute and the valid element/DOCTYPE scaffolding wholesale rather than byte-by-byte; the winning seeds were produced by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator and reach the false branch with an extreme hitcount of 1959 vs cmplog's 1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no structural generalization; it can substitute logged operands at matching offsets but cannot assemble or preserve the larger valid-XML structure needed to repeatedly drive the namespaced-attribute SAX path, so it stays blocked (8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural mutation (GeneralizationStage + Grimoire token/gap recombination) reassembles the namespaced-attribute structure that cmplog's I2S cannot build, vastly increasing reach of the prefix==NULL call.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is xmlNewNs's prefix-NULL test at tree.c:766, globally blocked on the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     if (prefix != NULL)
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The winning false branch is taken when xmlNewNs is called with a NULL prefix from the default-namespace SAX path.
The unprefixed xmlns attribute name is gated by a chained per-character equality cascade in the SAX attribute path.

scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1959  loser=1

folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is

evidence_trail.claim: The default-namespace branch in xmlNewNs is reached only by the winner-resolving seed set in the caller (1214).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating xmlNewNs on a multi-byte literal `xmlns` comparison cascade where value_profile does NOT outperform naive (no partial-progress credit advantage) and grimoire does NOT outperform cmplog — i.e. all four winners tie their losers — would refute that these techniques each independently enable the prefix==NULL path.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L divergence, so the precise input bytes that flip the branch are inferred from source/caller structure (the xmlns name cascade) rather than observed directly in the seed diff.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GAP-placeholder generalization and structural mutators splice prefixed-namespace XML fragments (e.g. '<b k:9') into well-formed documents wholesale, repeatedly producing prefixed namespace trees that cmplog's havoc+I2S stack builds only rarely.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices prefixed-namespace XML fragments to reach xmlFreeNs non-NULL prefix path

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must parse a well-formed XML namespace node carrying a non-NULL prefix so xmlFreeNs frees a prefixed ns

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same program-side condition (a parsed namespace node with non-NULL prefix freed by xmlFreeNs), but reached by structurally recombining XML token fragments — DOCTYPE/SYSTEM/element substructure including a prefixed element like '<b k:9' — into a fresh well-formed document.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with an enormous branch hitcount of 1959 (vs cmplog's 1); its winning seeds carry intact XML structure (' ', 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"') and its lineage mixes GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator with I2S, so structural splicing of generalized token/gap runs repeatedly reconstructs prefixed-namespace trees.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (which already has I2S, the single-technique delta being grimoire's added generalization + structural mutators) blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 1; its blocking seeds corrupt the document header bytes rather than recombining valid structural fragments, so it rarely reaches the namespace-free path.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, repeatedly producing well-formed XML containing prefixed namespaces — a capability cmplog's havoc+I2S stack lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the prefix-null check in xmlFreeNs, taken only when the freed namespace has a non-NULL prefix.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line:     if (cur->prefix != NULL) xmlFree((char *) cur->prefix);

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose namespace node is freed via xmlFreeNs but where value_profile/naive_ctx/grimoire resolve the false side no faster than their losers (equal time-to-coverage and equal branch hitcount), showing the divergence is not driven by reaching a prefixed-namespace free.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L divergence, so the prefixed-namespace ('k:') feature is inferred from winner seed structure rather than a clean per-offset W/L contrast.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and grammar-free structural mutators recombine generalized XML element/namespace fragments, reliably building nested structures with default-namespace declarations that cmplog's havoc+I2S assembles only accidentally.
signature.one_line: Grimoire recombines element/namespace fragments to reliably reach default-namespace nsDef

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must build XML carrying a default (unprefixed) namespace declaration xmlns="..." so xmlSearchNs hits a nsDef with prefix==NULL and nameSpace==NULL

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed XML document with elements bearing namespace declarations, reachable by recombining generalized token/gap structures rather than blind byte havoc.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with a very high branch hitcount (2199) and its winning seeds are produced by Grimoire structural mutators (seed 2 mutation_op=GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator,GrimoireExtensionMutator) that splice generalized XML fragments containing element/namespace structure, repeatedly reaching the default-namespace nsDef.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for grimoire) blocks 8/10 with hitcount 2 — without the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural recombination it does not reliably assemble the nested element structure that holds a default-namespace declaration.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus structural (grammar-free) mutators recombine generalized XML token/gap structures, building the well-formed default-namespace markup that cmplog's havoc+I2S stack assembles only rarely.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch requires cur->prefix==NULL, nameSpace==NULL, and cur->href!=NULL — a default-namespace nsDef looked up with a NULL prefix.
The winner returns at line 6184 (W only) while the loser returns at the prefixed-namespace line 6188 (L only).
At the blocker the winner takes the true side and the loser is entirely on the false side / a different prefixed compare.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L6182  T=735 F=0  T=0 F=180  if ((cur->prefix == NULL) && (nameSpace == NULL) &&  <-- BLOCKER

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a default-namespace lookup (prefix==NULL, nameSpace==NULL) where value_profile, grimoire, and naive_ctx fail to out-resolve their respective baselines (cmplog/naive) — i.e. all four involved winners block as often as their losers despite the gate being present.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level link is weak: BYTE DIFF reports no informative divergence, so the xmlns-token evidence in winning seeds is partial and the exact program-side condition is inferred from source plus per-role branch directions rather than a clean W-vs-L byte contrast.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators (Extension/RandomDelete) splice generalized XML token/gap structures into deep multi-namespace trees with prefixed nodes, which cmplog's byte-level I2S substitution cannot assemble.
signature.one_line: Grimoire structural recombination builds multi-namespace XML trees cmplog cannot

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching the false side needs a well-formed XML doc with element nodes carrying nsDef declarations and prefixed names so xmlSearchNs loops without an early match.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document (prolog + DOCTYPE + nested elements) whose element nodes carry namespace prefix declarations (e.g. a prefixed attribute like 'k:f='), so that xmlSearchNs enters the nsDef while-loop at 6181, evaluates a node whose cur->prefix is non-NULL, and falls through line 6185 (false side) because the prefix does not equal the searched nameSpace rather than matching and returning at 6188.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are deep, well-formed XML with prolog/DOCTYPE and nested tags including a prefixed name ('<a><b<b k:f='), built by Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalized token/gap structures; per-role coverage shows the winner taking the false-then-continue path (line 6184 [W] return, 6191-6201 [W] orig!=node block) and hitting the branch 30x on average.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds reach the true side instead (line 6188 [L] return at the inner match, and the L-only xmlNewNs/xmlNsWarnMsg path at SAX2.c 1741-1745), and several loser seeds are byte-corrupted prologs (leading 'fa 00 00 00' / 'f9 00 00 00' garbage, repeated '0f' runs, '//+'/*:'*ff' junk) that do not build the rich multi-namespace element tree required to iterate past a match; cmplog lacks the structural recombination to assemble the valid deep document.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator, GrimoireExtensionMutator) recombine generalized XML token/gap structures into deep well-formed documents with multiple namespace-prefixed nodes; cmplog (which shares the same I2S stage) only mutates bytes/tokens and cannot reliably synthesize the nested namespaced tree, so the divergence is attributable to the grimoire-structural technique, not I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the false side of the prefix/nameSpace equality test inside the nsDef loop of xmlSearchNs.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
The single decisive pair is grimoire over cmplog with the grimoire_structural delta.
The winner resolves the branch 9/10 trials while the loser blocks 8/10.
The winner hits the branch far more often than the loser (30 vs 1 average hitcount).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=30  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: A grimoire winner seed is a deep XML document with prolog, DOCTYPE and a prefixed name node.
The grimoire winner seed was produced by Grimoire structural mutators.
Per-role coverage shows the winner-only path through the orig!=node namespace block following the false fall-through.
Loser-blocking seeds reach the matching true-side return at 6188 instead of falling through.
Cmplog loser seeds have corrupted/garbage prologs rather than well-formed deep XML.
Grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack including I2SRandReplace and adds the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators as the single-technique delta.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures (string-based structural mutation).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog reaches the false-fall-through side as often as grimoire when the only varied axis is structural recombination of a deep namespaced XML tree (i.e., byte/token mutation alone suffices), showing grimoire structural mutation is not the divergence driver.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog cannot assemble the deep namespaced tree rests on garbage-prolog loser seeds; some loser seeds (e.g. d511ef109f42d209) do contain near-valid XML, so the structural barrier may be partial rather than absolute.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire generalizes corpus bytes into gap/token templates and recombines them, co-assembling a prefixed-namespace element (xmlns:prefix + prefix:localname + DTD ATTLIST) that requires multiple consistent structural fragments cmplog cannot synthesize.
signature.one_line: Grimoire recombines multi-fragment namespace structure cmplog cannot synthesize

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must build a prefixed namespaced element (prefix:local with a matching xmlns: declaration) so elem->ns and elem->ns->prefix are both non-NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document whose validated element carries a resolved namespace WITH a prefix: a 'prefix:localname' element name plus a matching 'xmlns:prefix' declaration so that the DTD-validation path computes elem->ns != NULL and elem->ns->prefix != NULL at valid.c:4071. This requires composing several co-dependent structural fragments (DOCTYPE/DTD with an ATTLIST, a namespace-declaring attribute, and a prefixed element) that must all be present and consistent in one input.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire generalizes corpus entries into <GAP> token structures and splices them, so it can recombine a known xmlns/prefix fragment with a prefixed-element fragment into one consistent document; the winning seed shows the assembled scaffold (xml prolog + DOCTYPE a SYSTEM 'dtds/127772.dtd') and grimoire resolves the branch in 8/10 trials with 414 avg hits on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S substitution and havoc/token mutation but no structural generalization/recombination, so it can solve individual byte comparisons but rarely assembles the multi-fragment prefixed-namespace structure; it blocks 8/10 trials with only 1 avg hit, and its loser seeds reach valid.c:4071 but always take the false side (T=0 F=33).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers run the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so comparison-solving is not the delta; grimoire's single-technique advantage is the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (string-based, grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures), which build the consistent prefixed-namespace document the gate at line 4071 needs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch requires the validated element to have a namespace with a non-NULL prefix.
The single-technique delta between grimoire and cmplog is grimoire's generalization plus structural mutators, since both already have I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization + Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The winning seed contains a well-formed XML prolog and a DOCTYPE referencing an external DTD, the structural scaffold needed for DTD-validated attribute normalization.
The loser seeds reach valid.c:4071 but never take the true (blocking) side.

if ((elem->ns != NULL) && (elem->ns->prefix != NULL)) {  <-- BLOCKER

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winning seed snippet shows only the XML prolog/DOCTYPE preamble (first 64 bytes), not the prefixed xmlns: element bytes, so the prefixed-namespace structure is inferred from the source gate rather than directly visible in the cited seed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators splice generalized XML token structures to produce bare xmlns (no-prefix) namespace declarations repeatedly, taking the false side at the prefix==NULL check; cmplog's havoc/I2S mutations never assemble the required default-namespace element.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices default-namespace (no-prefix) XML declarations cmplog cannot assemble

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must declare a DEFAULT namespace (xmlns with no prefix, cur->prefix==NULL) so serialization writes "xmlns" not "xmlns:".

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (a default namespace declaration so cur->prefix==NULL at line 603), but reached by structural recombination: a generalized XML token/gap structure spliced to place a bare xmlns (no prefix) on an element.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false side in 10/10 trials with a very high branch hitcount (625), consistent with its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators repeatedly splicing generalized markup tokens into many valid default-namespace declarations that all flow into xmlNsDumpOutput with prefix==NULL.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for this pair, sharing I2S but lacking generalization + Grimoire mutators) stays blocked 8/10 with branch hitcount 0; its byte-level havoc+I2S mutations do not assemble the structural default-namespace element needed to take the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, building syntactically valid XML that carries a prefix-less (default) namespace declaration, which byte-level cmplog mutation does not reach.

At the blocker line the winner role takes the false side while the loser role only takes the true side.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the only namespace form reachable is a default (prefix-less) namespace, yet naive/cmplog still fail to resolve the false branch while value_profile/grimoire do — showing the divergence is not driven by the prefix==NULL structural feature but by some unrelated property.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP sequence whose Hamming/prefix-distance gradient value_profile exploits to construct a prefix==NULL namespace is inferred from the source path, not directly observed in a value_profile winner seed's lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural string/gap mutators preserve and recombine generalized prefixed-element token structures (e.g. `<b k:f="">`) producing valid prefix:localname elements; cmplog's byte-level havoc never reliably builds a namespaced element so cur->ns->prefix stays NULL.
signature.one_line: Grimoire recombines prefixed-element token structures to keep cur->ns->prefix non-null

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must produce a well-formed namespaced element (e.g. prefix:name) so the closing-tag dump sees cur->ns->prefix != NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: XML containing an element that is bound to a namespace WITH a prefix and is fully serialized (open + children-or-empty + close), so the writer reaches the closing-tag emit at line 1050 with cur->ns->prefix != NULL. This needs a valid prefix:localname construction plus a matching xmlns:prefix declaration, e.g. the grimoire winner's '<b k:f="">' style prefixed token inside a DOCTYPE/element structure.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire's generalization + structural string mutators recombine the generalized DOCTYPE/element token structure to keep emitting syntactically valid prefixed elements: winner seed 1 contains the prefixed construct '<b k:f="">' and the per-role overlay shows grimoire (W) actually takes the true side of the namespace-prefix check at the opening tag (line 872 W=T=7) and reaches the closing-tag blocker with W resolving 8/10 trials and hitcount 606 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog (havoc + I2S, no structural generalization) almost never assembles a namespaced element: at the opening-tag namespace-prefix check line 872 the loser shows T=0 F=73 (zero true), so cur->ns->prefix is never non-NULL, and the closing-tag blocker line 1050 shows L T=0 with the loser blocked 8/10 trials and hitcount 0 on the branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire share the input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so the divergence is NOT comparison-solving; the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural string/gap mutators, which preserve and recombine the generalized XML token structure to repeatedly produce a valid prefixed namespaced element that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot reliably build.

The winner is grimoire and the loser is cmplog, with the single-technique delta being grimoire_structural.

At the blocker line 1050 the loser takes the true side zero times.
Grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural string mutators on top of the full cmplog (I2S) stack, so I2S is shared and the delta is structural.

The single-technique delta is therefore

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog resolves the prefixed-closing-tag branch as often as grimoire (no significant grimoire>cmplog gap) when the only required feature is a valid namespaced element with a prefix.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly attribute the resolving input to grimoire's GeneralizationStage versus its structural mutators since lineage names are empty for grimoire, only that the leaf seed is a GrimoireExtensionMutator product.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators splice grammar-preserving token runs to keep a well-formed DOCTYPE and prefixed attribute intact, reaching the namespace-prefix fullelemname branch; cmplog corrupts DTD references, preventing valid ns/prefix element processing.
signature.one_line: Grimoire preserves well-formed DOCTYPE+prefixed attr reaching ns fullelemname branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed XML doc with a valid DTD DOCTYPE and a namespace-prefixed element/attribute that survives SAX2 attribute processing into xmlIsID's else-arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed XML document: a parseable `<?xml ...?>` prolog, a `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">` declaration that resolves an internal/external subset, and an element with a namespace-prefixed attribute (e.g. `<b k:f="">`) so that elem!=NULL reaches valid.c:2791 and constructs fullelemname via xmlBuildQName.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact grammar tokens: `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">` followed by `<a> <b k:f="">`, a prefixed attribute name that drives ns/prefix-bearing element processing all the way through SAX2 into xmlIsID's else-arm (line 2791 True side hit 8 times by W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds have corrupted/truncated DTD references (`dtdskk`, `dtds;1`, `dtds/1`-truncated) or a mangled prolog (`eeeeeTTLIST`), so the document is not well-formed or the DTD does not resolve; xmlIsID is reached but only its early-return paths execute (line 2791 True never taken, only the no-prefix False side at hit-count 39 vs 8).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts the corpus XML into token/gap structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtensionMutator etc.) splice those generalised grammar fragments to assemble a still-well-formed document with a resolvable DTD plus a prefixed element/attribute; plain cmplog (havoc+I2S, which grimoire also has) cannot maintain whole-document grammar validity across mutations, so the single-technique delta is grimoire structural mutation, not I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog with the grimoire_structural technique delta, the only decisive pair at this branch.
The blocker is the true (prefixed) side of the fullelemname conditional, reached only inside xmlIsID's else-arm where elem!=NULL.
Winner seeds contain a well-formed DOCTYPE/DTD declaration and a namespace-prefixed attribute structure.
Winner seed bytes spell a prefixed attribute element b k:f used to build the qualified element name.
Loser seeds have corrupted DTD references that break document well-formedness / DTD resolution.
Another loser seed has a mangled prolog token rather than a valid declaration.
The winner takes the true branch at line 2791 while the loser only takes the false branch, with W reaching the prefixed-name path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2791  T=8 F=0  T=0 F=39  fullelemname = (elem->ns != NULL && elem->ns->prefix != N...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire adds GeneralizationStage and structural mutators on top of the full cmplog I2S stack, so the technique delta is structural mutation not I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures in a grammar-free structural way.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The winning seed was produced by a Grimoire structural mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the only path to the blocker requires a well-formed DTD-backed document with a prefixed element name shows cmplog resolving the branch at parity with grimoire (no grimoire>cmplog gap), indicating grammar-preserving structural mutation is not the discriminating factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the failure mode for cmplog is genuinely DTD/well-formedness corruption versus simply not yet reaching the deep SAX2 attribute path; the byte-diff shows broad divergence rather than a single isolatable corrupted offset.
